_note: D3.C.2 failure-mode example #3 — legacy UK office refurbishment with 50W halogen downlights (750 lm, 15 lm/W lamp efficacy). is_uk_new_build=true because major refurbishment triggers Part L 2021 §6. Lamp efficacy 15 lm/W ≪ 95 lm/W target → INV-6 FAIL critical. Plus no daylight-linking despite glazed W wall + no occupancy detection → INV-6 sub-checks 2+3 also FAIL. INV-10 composite FAIL. INV-1 still PASSes (343 lux ≥ 300) because the engineer over-provisioned fittings to compensate for halogen's low efficacy.
_d5_retrofit_note: Retrofitted by D5 sprint 2026-06-04. v1.6.0 → v1.7.0: existing single zone Z2 defaults to purpose='task' per ZP-01 backwards-compat rule (BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.2.2.1); all 50 GU10 halogen downlights default to mount_type='recessed' per MT-01 (recessed downlight is recessed by definition); per_zone_achieved[] populated from existing achieved_illuminance_lux=343.75 lx mapped to Z2 task target=300 lx. Engineering content unchanged — Part-L FAIL state (efficacy 15<<95 + no daylight + no occupancy + INV-6/INV-10 FAIL + 3 critical non_compliance_flags + controls.part_l_compliant=false) is PRESERVED VERBATIM because D5 additions (zone purpose + mount_type + per-zone achievement) are orthogonal to Part L 2021 §6 efficacy/controls compliance. The photometric INV-11 PASS + INV-19 PASS coexist with the regulatory INV-6 FAIL — this is the canonical 'photometrically compliant but regulatorily non-compliant' refurb. Honest disclosure mirrored in output.calculation_summary.assumptions[], output.rationale.sections[], and reasoning.md §D5.

luminaire.type: 50W GU10 halogen recessed downlight — legacy refurbishment

photometric_ies_paths._source: Synthetic 50W GU10 halogen downlight, 2800K, 36-degree beam (legacy retrofit demo for uk-part-l-fail-incandescent). 15 lm/W efficacy, fails Approved Doc L 2021 §6 Table 6.2 95 lm/W minimum. verification_status: synthetic_reference_C3. Cascade retrofit per photometric-analysis v1.0 sprint D.2; engineer-of-record substitutes project IES before final design freeze.
